buginformation.summary: cxf-jaxrs-security publish url need to be changed to securecrm
buginformation.description: As cxf-jaxrs is using the url crm, cxf-jaxrs-security need to choice other name for it.

buginformation.summary: Remove obsolete feature 'activemq-broker'
buginformation.description:
The feature activemq-broker is no longer useful - the Blueprint configuration file referenced in the feature is not available in the assembly:
FuseESB:karaf@root> features:info activemq-broker
Description of activemq-broker 4.5.0.fuse-7-0-068 feature
----------------------------------------------------------------
Feature has no configuration
Feature has no configuration files
Feature depends on:
activemq-blueprint 5.5.1.fuse-7-0-068
Feature contains followed bundles:
blueprint:file:etc/activemq-broker.xml start-level=50

buginformation.summary: the shell help for the patch:install command is wrong
buginformation.description: The help for patch:install reads: ARGUMENTS PATCH name of the patch to rollback It should be name of patch to install

buginformation.summary: activemq-camel - This feature is not listed as installed out of the box in the full-esb
buginformation.description:
Run this command
features:list -i
To show installed features. Notice that the activemq-camel feature is not installed. Instead there is a camel-activemq which is from SMX installed ] [4.5.0.fuse-71-036 ] camel-activemq servicemix-4.5.0.fuse-71-036 Then I install the correct feature
features:install activemq-camel
And list all the features again
features:list -i
And now we have both
[installed ] [4.5.0.fuse-71-036 ] camel-activemq servicemix-4.5.0.fuse-71-036
[installed ] [5.7.0.fuse-71-036 ] activemq activemq-5.7.0.fuse-71-036
[installed ] [5.7.0.fuse-71-036 ] activemq-spring activemq-5.7.0.fuse-71-036
[installed ] [5.7.0.fuse-71-036 ] activemq-blueprint activemq-5.7.0.fuse-71-036
[installed ] [5.7.0.fuse-71-036 ] activemq-camel activemq-5.7.0.fuse-71-036
I am not sure if that camel-activemq feature should be installed? At least the activemq-camel should be, as that&apos;s the correct feature for integrating ActiveMQ and Camel.

buginformation.summary: Can not install patches as shell script
buginformation.description:
When trying to install PATCH-3, we have a problem using: ./client -u smx -p smx patch:install PATCH-3 Essentially the patch begins to install but then hangs. Further discussions seem to indicate a problem with with the ssh client restarting. Test Case Steps: wildflower:bin sjavurek$ ./client -u smx -p smx patch:list [name] [installed] [description] PATCH-3 false null wildflower:bin sjavurek$ ./client -u smx -p smx patch:install PATCH-3 The second command just hangs. ESB never shuts down and I can see in the log that it appears to be shutting down:
17:26:10,940 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | NamespacePlugins | 106 - org.springframework.osgi.extender - 1.2.1 | Removing handler Apache Karaf :: Admin :: Command (org.apache.karaf.admin.command)
17:26:10,940 | DEBUG | lixDispatchQueue | command | 30 - org.apache.karaf.admin.command - 2.2.5.fuse-70-097 | BundleEvent STOPPED
17:26:10,941 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | Activator | 141 - org.apache.camel.camel-core - 2.9.0.fuse-70-097 | Bundle stopped: org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh
17:26:10,941 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | BlueprintExtender | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Destroying BlueprintContainer for bundle org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh
17:26:10,941 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | BlueprintEventDispatcher | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Sending blueprint container event BlueprintEvent[type=DESTROYING] for bundle org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh
17:26:10,942 | DEBUG | nt Dispatcher: 1 | BlueprintListener | 39 - org.apache.karaf.shell.osgi - 2.2.5.fuse-70-097 | Blueprint app state changed to Destroying for bundle 23
17:26:10,942 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ssh | 23 - org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh - 2.2.5.fuse-70-097 | ServiceEvent UNREGISTERING
17:26:10,943 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ServiceRecipe | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Unregistering service shell-2
17:26:10,943 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ServiceRecipe | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Calling listeners for service unregistration
17:26:10,944 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ssh | 23 - org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh - 2.2.5.fuse-70-097 | ServiceEvent UNREGISTERING
17:26:10,945 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ServiceRecipe | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Unregistering service shell-4
17:26:10,945 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ServiceRecipe | 10 - org.apache.aries.blueprint - 0.3.1.fuse-70-097 | Calling listeners for service unregistration
17:26:10,945 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ssh | 23 - org.apache.karaf.shell.ssh - 2.2.5.fuse-70-097 | ServiceEvent UNREGISTERING
17:26:10,948 | INFO | NioProcessor-5 | ServerSession | 26 - sshd-core - 0.5.0 | Closing session
17:26:10,948 | DEBUG | NioProcessor-5 | ServerSession | 26 - sshd-core - 0.5.0 | Closing channel 0
17:26:10,948 | INFO | NioProcessor-5 | ChannelSession | 26 - sshd-core - 0.5.0 | Send SSH_MSG_CHANNEL_CLOSE on channel 0
From here the install process just hangs and servicemix is unreachable:
wildflower:bin sjavurek$ ./client -u smx -p smx
Failed to get the session.
I&apos;ve also attached the log. Workaround Ideas Would be to open the session and install the patch, however, that is manual and in automated fashion not feasible. Fabric is another alternative but for this issue we need an automated, non fabric solution. Susan

buginformation.summary: Feature dependencies not being installed in Fabric
Camel Master Component Ignoring parameters
Camel Fabric component doesn't take the parameters into consideration
buginformation.description: camel fabric component doesn&apos;t pass the parameters to the client endpoint which is same with the CAMEL-522.

buginformation.summary: ER1: Error when creating child container with "default" profile (-007 kit)
buginformation.description: There is NullPointerException and other Exceptions are thrown when you try to create child container with "default" profile and with some profiles which extend it. Basically it&apos;s not possible to create the child container with almost every profile (jboss-fuse-* profiles are working ok). Attaching log file.

buginformation.summary: ER1: Warning building demos - redhat-007
buginformation.description: C:\Fuse\jboss-fuse-6.0.0.redhat-007\examples\eip>mvn clean install [INFO] Scanning for projects... [WARNING] [WARNING] Some problems were encountered while building the effective model for org.fusesource.examples:eip:jar:6.0.0.redhat-007 [WARNING] &apos;parent.relativePath&apos; points at org.jboss.fuse:project instead of org. fusesource.examples:project, please verify your project structure @ line 26, col umn 13 [WARNING] [WARNING] It is highly recommended to fix these problems because they threaten t he stability of your build. [WARNING] [WARNING] For this reason, future Maven versions might no longer support buildin g such malformed projects. [WARNING] [INFO] [INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------ [INFO] Building JBoss Fuse :: Examples :: Enterprise Integration Patterns 6.0.0. redhat-007 [INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------ [INFO]

buginformation.summary: ER1: rest example (and other examples) - wrong resources package
buginformation.description: In "rest" example there is wrong package to resources which are provided with this example. This problem results in NullPointerException in rest Client class: This line throws NPE: String inputFile = client.getClass().getResource("update_customer.xml").getFile(); Wrong path: rest/src/main/resources/org/fusesource/examples/cxf/jaxrs/client/ Should be: rest/src/main/resources/org/jboss/fuse/examples/cxf/jaxrs/client/

buginformation.summary: Null Pointer if invalid patch command syntax is used
buginformation.description:
The following error messages occur if invalid syntax is used when applying a patch to a standalone ESB container:
FuseESB:karaf@root> patch:list
[name] [installed] [description]
fuse-esb-7.1.0.fuse-047-p3 false null
FuseESB:karaf@root>
FuseESB:karaf@root> patch:simulate
Error executing command: Patch &apos;null&apos; not found
FuseESB:karaf@root>
FuseESB:karaf@root> patch:install
Error executing command: Patch &apos;null&apos; not found
FuseESB:karaf@root>
A more informative message should be displayed to indicate a patch name should be supplied to the command.

buginformation.summary: cxf-codefirst archetype has an error
buginformation.description: see ECLIPSE-885 The package declaration inside OrderTest.java is hardcoded to "package camelinaction;" and not affected by package definition when creating the archetype.

buginformation.summary: In Fabric you can assign same profile to container more than once
buginformation.description: You can assign one profile to one container more than once. Steps to reproduce: 1. fabric:create 2. First: fabric:container-add-profile root example-mq 3. Again: fabric:container-add-profile root example-mq 4. fabric:container-list Output: [id] [version] [alive] [profiles] [provision status] root* 1.0 true example-mq, example-mq, fabric, fabric-ensemble-0000-1, jboss-fuse-full success There is example-mq profile twice in list.

buginformation.summary: Message 'New user name:' is printed to terminal continuously
buginformation.description: Message &apos;New user name:&apos; is printed to terminal continuously if Ctrl+d is pressed after command fabric:create is executed without any users in etc/users.properties specified. Steps to reproduce: 1. Don&apos;t specify any users in in the file jboss-fuse-6.0.0.redhat-023/etc/user.properties 2. Run jboss-fuse v023 3. Run the following command: fabric:create 4. After message &apos;New user name:&apos; appears, press Ctrl+d Result: Message &apos;New user name:&apos; is printed to terminal continuously.

buginformation.summary: fab ignore the proxy configuration in settings.xml
pax-url does not handle escaped characters in url.
buginformation.description: Consider a maven repo url like http://user%40domain.org:password@localhost:8081/artifactory/libs-release@id=artifactory added to etc/org.ops4j.pax.url.cfg. The url contains also sets the username (user@domain.org) and password (password) credentials to be used. user@domain.org is correctly escaped using %40. Trying to resolve an artifact from that repository however fails. pax-url simply Base 64 encodes the credentials "user%40domain.org:password", rather than "user@domain.org:password". On the server side the user user%40domain.org is unknown (whereas a user user@domain.org is known) and so a 401 Bad credentials HTTP error is returned. Pax-url should un-escape these characters (%40 and others) before trying to use the url. I hope the list at http://www.w3schools.com/tags/ref_urlencode.asp is fairly exhaustive.

buginformation.summary: Invalid parent profile of jboss-fuse-full profile
buginformation.description: "jboss-fuse-medium" profile which is parent of "jboss-fuse-full" profile has as a parent "amq" profile, which doesn&apos;t exists. It should be "mq-amq"

buginformation.summary: Cannot visualize child container info from hawtio
buginformation.description: Using jboss-fuse-6.1.0.redhat-114, when we try to create a container with profile jboss-medium, jboss-full .... then the following error appears on the screen (Hawtio) even if the container is well created : JBossFuse:karaf@root> fabric:container-create-child --profile jboss-fuse-full root demo Jmx Login for root: admin Jmx Password for root: The following containers have been created successfully: Container: demo. JBossFuse:karaf@root> fabric:container-list [id] [version] [alive] [profiles] [provision status] root* 1.0 true fabric, fabric-ensemble-0000-1 success demo 1.0 true jboss-fuse-full See gist error : https://gist.github.com/cmoulliard/6914237

buginformation.summary: Inconsistency of creating fabric from Hawtio and Shell Console
buginformation.description: When creating fabric through shell console the profiles assigned to container are: fabric, fabric-ensemble-0000-1 In this case ActiveMQ is stopped When creating fabric through Hawtio the profiles assigned to root container are: fabric, fabric-ensemble-0000-1, fabric, hawtio, jboss-fuse-full In this case ActiveMQ is still running Which behavior is correct?

buginformation.summary: Error when installing jpa-hibernate
buginformation.description: When I try: features:install jpa-hibernate I get: Error executing command: Jar is not a bundle, no Bundle-SymbolicName mvn:org.springframework/spring-jdbc/3.2.4.RELEASE

buginformation.summary: Error importing files into fabric using CLI
buginformation.description: Trying to import broker config into a newly created fabric results in the following error... Error executing command: Path must not end with / character When running in debug I can see that the path causing the stacktrace below is.. /fabric/configs/versions/1.0/profiles/mq-base/my-broker.xml/ Looks like a forward slash is being appended to the target path. Stacktrace.. 13:39:06,307 | INFO | l Console Thread | Console | 16 - org.apache.karaf.shell.console - 2.3.0.redhat-610321 | Exception caught while executing command java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Path must not end with / character at org.apache.zookeeper.common.PathUtils.validatePath(PathUtils.java:58)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321] at org.apache.zookeeper.ZooKeeper.exists(ZooKeeper.java:1020)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321] at org.apache.curator.framework.imps.ExistsBuilderImpl$2.call(ExistsBuilderImpl.java:172)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321] at org.apache.curator.framework.imps.ExistsBuilderImpl$2.call(ExistsBuilderImpl.java:161)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321] at org.apache.curator.RetryLoop.callWithRetry(RetryLoop.java:107)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321] at org.apache.curator.framework.imps.ExistsBuilderImpl.pathInForeground(ExistsBuilderImpl.java:157)[56:io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-321]

buginformation.summary: NPE when installing the first patch on a standalone container
buginformation.description:
When installing the first patch created with the script in ENTESB-1154, I noticed this NPE on the console.
java.lang.NullPointerException
at java.util.Hashtable.put(Hashtable.java:542)
at org.fusesource.patch.impl.ServiceImpl.saveResult(ServiceImpl.java:286)
at org.fusesource.patch.impl.ServiceImpl$1.run(ServiceImpl.java:435)
This appears to be caused by the fact that there&apos;s no override.properties before installing the first patch, causing this NPE when the null value for the overrides is written to the properties file.

buginformation.summary: Fabric Karaf Help command does not display something
buginformation.description:
fabric:create --help don&apos;t display anymore the description, syntax and options of the command
100% [========================================================================]
_ ____ ______
| | _ \ | ____|
| | |_) | ___ ___ ___ | |__ _ _ ___ ___
_ | | _ < / _ \/ __/ __| | __| | | / __|/ _ \
| |__| | |_) | (_) \__ \__ \ | | | |_| \__ \ __/
\____/|____/ \___/|___/___/ |_| \__,_|___/\___|
JBoss Fuse (6.1.0.redhat-346)
http://www.redhat.com/products/jbossenterprisemiddleware/fuse/
Hit &apos;<tab>&apos; for a list of available commands
and &apos;[cmd] --help&apos; for help on a specific command.
Open a browser to http://localhost:8181 to access the management console
Create a new Fabric via &apos;fabric:create&apos;
or join an existing Fabric via &apos;fabric:join [someUrls]&apos;
Hit &apos;<ctrl-d>&apos; or &apos;osgi:shutdown&apos; to shutdown JBoss Fuse.
JBossFuse:karaf@root> fabric:create --help
JBossFuse:karaf@root>

buginformation.summary: patch:simulate should not update etc/startup.properties
buginformation.description:
While running patch:simulate to look into a problem with the patching mechanism, I noticed this output:
JBossFuse:karaf@root> patch:simulate fabric-zookeeper-patch
DEBUG: Applying patch: fabric-zookeeper-patch / null
DEBUG: Adding artifact: mvn:io.fabric8/fabric-zookeeper/1.0.0.redhat-35X
DEBUG: Overwriting startup.properties with: io.fabric8:fabric-zookeeper:1.0.0.redhat-35X
DEBUG: Adding artifact: mvn:io.fabric8/fabric-zookeeper-commands/1.0.0.redhat-35X
Bundles to update:
io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper/1.0.0.redhat-350 with mvn:io.fabric8/fabric-zookeeper/1.0.0.redhat-35X
Installation will begin. The connection may be lost or the console restarted.
[name] [old] [new]
io.fabric8.fabric-zookeeper 1.0.0.redhat-350 1.0.0.redhat-35X
It looks like the simulate step already updated my etc/startup.properties file.

buginformation.summary: Libraries in profile cause container deployment to fail
buginformation.description: Adding the following library configuration to example-camel-sap profile causes deployment of child &apos;sap&apos; container to fail: lib.sapjco3.jar = file:///Users/wcollins/Dev/sapjco3-darwinintel64-3.0.8/sapjco3.jar lib.libsapjco3.jnilib = file:///Users/wcollins/Dev/sapjco3-darwinintel64-3.0.8/libsapjco3.jnilib The following exception is observed in child &apos;sap&apos; container log: 2014-02-10 16:26:07,844 | ERROR | pool-36-thread-1 | ConfigInjection | .api.scr.support.ConfigInjection 51 | 59 - io.fabric8.fabric-api - 1.0.0.redhat-328 | Failed to inject configuration {service.pid=io.fabric8.datastore, type=caching-git, component.name=io.fabric8.datastore, component.id=34, gitPullPeriod=1000} into io.fabric8.git.internal.CachingGitDataStore@185e093f java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 0 at io.fabric8.api.scr.support.ConfigInjection.normalizePropertyName(ConfigInjection.java:171) at io.fabric8.api.scr.support.ConfigInjection.injectMetaTypePropertyValue(ConfigInjection.java:142) at io.fabric8.api.scr.support.ConfigInjection.applyConfiguration(ConfigInjection.java:91) at io.fabric8.api.scr.support.ConfigInjection.applyConfiguration(ConfigInjection.java:47) at io.fabric8.git.internal.CachingGitDataStore.activate(CachingGitDataStore.java:98) at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)[:1.6.0_65] at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:39)[:1.6.0_65] at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:25)[:1.6.0_65] at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:597)[:1.6.0_65] at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:695)[:1.6.0_65]

buginformation.summary: HTTP/FTP Deployment URL's for Libraries are not supported
buginformation.description: HTTP/FTP deployment URL&apos;s for libraries do not resolve when placed in profiles; file deployment URL&apos;s for libraries however do resolve. A network deployment URL will be required in order to support deployment of libraries across the distributed fabric environment.

buginformation.summary: Create dummy patch jars to test patch
buginformation.description: Hi, WE need to create some sort of test jars to test the patching capabilities of 6.1. The following areas should be covered: 1. Fabric container 2. Non-fabric container I don&apos;t think this needs to be fancy. I&apos;m thinking of some jars that when installed/started print a message to the log which we can grep. Hopefully we can automate part of the test. Probably best to do some checks in Fabric itself, MQ, Camel.

buginformation.summary: connector features does not install
buginformation.description:
features:install connector give me:
2014-11-18 15:47:47,680 | ERROR | l Console Thread | Console | ? ? | 28 - org.apache.karaf.shell.console - 2.4.0.redhat-620047 | Exception caught while executing command
java.lang.Exception: Can not resolve feature:
Unsatisfied requirement(s):
---------------------------
package:(&(package=javax.resource.spi)(version>=1.6.0)(!(version>=2.0.0)))
Geronimo TxManager :: Connector
package:(&(package=javax.resource)(version>=1.6.0)(!(version>=2.0.0)))
Geronimo TxManager :: Connector
package:(&(package=javax.resource.spi.endpoint)(version>=1.6.0)(!(version>=2.0.0)))
Geronimo TxManager :: Connector
package:(&(package=javax.resource.spi.work)(version>=1.6.0)(!(version>=2.0.0)))
Geronimo TxManager :: Connector
at org.apache.karaf.features.obr.internal.ObrResolver.resolve(ObrResolver.java:129)[12:org.apache.karaf.features.obr:2.4.0.redhat-620047]
at org.apache.karaf.features.internal.FeaturesServiceImpl.resolve(FeaturesServiceImpl.java:717)[9:org.apache.karaf.features.core:2.4.0.redhat-620047]
at org.apache.karaf.features.internal.FeaturesServiceImpl.doInstallFeature(FeaturesServiceImpl.java:672)[9:org.apache.karaf.features.core:2.4.0.redhat-620047]
at org.apache.karaf.features.internal.FeaturesServiceImpl.doInstallFeatures(FeaturesServiceImpl.java:432)[9:org.apache.karaf.features.core:2.4.0.redhat-620047]
at org.apache.karaf.features.internal.FeaturesServiceImpl$1.call(FeaturesServiceImpl.java:402)[9:org.apache.karaf.features.core:2.4.0.redhat-620047]
at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:266)[:1.8.0_20]
at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1142)[:1.8.0_20]
at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:617)[:1.8.0_20]
at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:745)[:1.8.0_20]

buginformation.summary: [RH-Support] SUPPORT attachement populated only in case of a Fabric environment
buginformation.description: SUPPORT archive is empty in case of not fabric environemnt. I have to verify if it depends on OSGi dependencies and eventually if it&apos;s the case to decouple between fabric and no-fabric

buginformation.summary: Unmarshalling of IDoc Document List does not initialize root segment class
buginformation.description: When unmarshaling IDoc Document List the internal filed rootSegmentClass is not initialized resulting in Null Pointer exception when attempting to send list through SAP IDoc Document List endpoint.

buginformation.summary: ABAP 'SPACE' keyword assigned as default value in JCo meta-data instead of space character
buginformation.description: ABAP defines the keyword SPACE to specify a single space character in ABAP programs. This keyword is used regularly to specify the default value of a single character CHAR parameter in remote function module interfaces. The meta data for this parameter from the JCo runtime will indicate the default value for it using the keyword (i.e. literally the character string &apos;SPACE&apos;) instead of the character string &apos; &apos;. This causes the data layer of the SAP components to use the character string &apos;SPACE&apos; for the value of the parameter instead of &apos; &apos; when the parameter is not specified.

buginformation.summary: Valid XML Documents for IDocs and SAP Structures failing to unmarshal
buginformation.description: XML Documents containing the serialized contents of SAP IDocs and Structure are failing to unmarshal. This occurs only when a Data Layer Run time object of given type has never been created before.

buginformation.summary: SAP Camel Component needs to unregister its Data Providers in OSGi environment
buginformation.description: The SAP Camel Component must register a pair of Data Providers with the JCo3 runtime&apos;s &apos;Environment&apos; singleton object in order to provide connection configurations. Only one pair of Data Provider objects can be registered in a JVM at one time. To enable reinstalling the SAP Camel Component bundle, the component bundle must unregister its data provider objects when being uninstalled s.t. component bundle can reinstall new data provider objects when it is reinstalled.

buginformation.summary: SAP Camel Component Unit Tests Failing
SAP Camel Server Components needs to stop and release JCo Servers
buginformation.description: SAP Camel Server Components needs to stop and release JCo Servers when application bundle is uninstalled. If not subsequent installs of application bundles will not install/reinstall.

buginformation.summary: Compile errors in camel-sap component
buginformation.description: The camel-sap component gets several compile errors because of the addition of title to the UrlEndpoint annotation in CAMEL-8558. I will add a temporary fix so we can get perfectus to run and then turn this over to Bill.

buginformation.summary: SAP Camel Server Components do not return responses
buginformation.description: The server components do not return responses to SAP caller.

buginformation.summary: Camel SAP - Spring container - Could not initialize class org.eclipse.emf.ecore.xml.type.XMLTypePackage
buginformation.description:
It is not possible to convert SAP request from String object to org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.model.rfc.Structure object.
Error during type conversion from type: java.lang.String to the required type: org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.model.rfc.Structure
...
Caused by: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: Could not initialize class org.eclipse.emf.ecore.xml.type.XMLTypePackage
at org.eclipse.emf.ecore.xmi.impl.XMLHelperImpl.<init>(XMLHelperImpl.java:85)

buginformation.summary: EsbProfileRedeployTest fails with java.lang.SecurityException: Insufficient credentials.
buginformation.description: This test has been failing regularly with the exception below. I will add an @Ignore so pull request builds can complete. java.lang.SecurityException: Insufficient credentials. at org.apache.karaf.service.guard.impl.GuardProxyCatalog$ProxyInvocationListener.preInvoke(GuardProxyCatalog.java:529) at org.apache.aries.proxy.impl.ProxyHandler$1.invoke(ProxyHandler.java:52) at org.apache.aries.proxy.impl.ProxyHandler.invoke(ProxyHandler.java:119) at io.fabric8.commands.$ContainerConnect1559360197.execute(Unknown Source) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.CommandProxy.execute(CommandProxy.java:78) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.Closure.executeCmd(Closure.java:477) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.Closure.executeStatement(Closure.java:403) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.Pipe.run(Pipe.java:108) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.Closure.execute(Closure.java:183) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.Closure.execute(Closure.java:120) at org.apache.felix.gogo.runtime.CommandSessionImpl.execute(CommandSessionImpl.java:92) at io.fabric8.tooling.testing.pax.exam.karaf.FabricKarafTestSupport$1$1.run(FabricKarafTestSupport.java:220) at io.fabric8.tooling.testing.pax.exam.karaf.FabricKarafTestSupport$1$1.run(FabricKarafTestSupport.java:206) at java.security.AccessController.doPrivileged(Native Method) at javax.security.auth.Subject.doAs(Subject.java:356) at io.fabric8.tooling.testing.pax.exam.karaf.FabricKarafTestSupport$1.call(FabricKarafTestSupport.java:206) at io.fabric8.tooling.testing.pax.exam.karaf.FabricKarafTestSupport$1.call(FabricKarafTestSupport.java:194) at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:262) at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:471) at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:262) at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1145) at java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:615) at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:745)

buginformation.summary: Bug in meta-data for sap-idoclist-server endpoint affecting Karaf console help
buginformation.description:
In the following file:
org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.SapTransactionalIDocListServerEndpoint
The @UriEndpoint annotation gives the wrong syntax:
@UriEndpoint(scheme = "sap-idoclist-server", consumerClass =
SapTransactionalIDocListConsumer.class, syntax =
"sap-idoclist-server:server:rfc", consumerOnly = true, title="SAP IDoc
List Server")
Presumably, the syntax is probably meant to be something like (taking the @UriPath fields into account):
syntax = "sap-idoclist-server:serverName:idocType:idocTypeExtension:systemRelease:applicationRelease"

buginformation.summary: Move Repository Data Stores to SAP Configuration to support Blueprint
buginformation.description: The repository data stores for SAP components do not properly initialize in Blueprint containers and should be moved into the central SAP Configuration object where connection configurations are maintained.

buginformation.summary: Fuse camel-sap SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest test fails
buginformation.description: The SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest is failing on Jenkins with the error below. I will add an @Ignore to allow the build to proceed for now. Stacktrace java.lang.AssertionError: null at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:86) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:41) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:52) at org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest.testComponentConfiguration(SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest.java:45) Standard Output [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO ******************************************************************************** [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO Testing: testComponentConfiguration(org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest) [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO ******************************************************************************** [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO ******************************************************************************** [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO Testing done: testComponentConfiguration(org.fusesource.camel.component.sap.SapTransactionalIDocListServerComponentConfigurationAndDocumentationTest) [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO Took: 0.127 seconds (127 millis) [ main] figurationAndDocumentationTest INFO ******************************************************************************** [ main] DefaultCamelContext INFO Apache Camel 2.15.1.redhat-620124 (CamelContext: camel-1) is shutting down [ main] DefaultCamelContext INFO Apache Camel 2.15.1.redhat-620124 (CamelContext: camel-1) uptime not started [ main] DefaultCamelContext INFO Apache Camel 2.15.1.redhat-620124 (CamelContext: camel-1) is shutdown in 0.005 seconds

buginformation.summary: Quickstarts should build without warnings; most should work without fabric
buginformation.description: The first quickstart that beginners are directed to is camel-log. Would it be better if 1st quickstart was one without fabric? Running camel-log following instructions in README gives lots of warnings then fails wilth following error [INFO] No tests to run. [INFO] [INFO] maven-bundle-plugin:2.3.7:bundle (default-bundle) @ beginner-camel-lo g [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Instructions in Export-Package that are never used: io\.fabric8\..*|io\ .fabric8 Classpath: Jar:.,Jar:camel-blueprint,Jar:camel-core,Jar:slf4j-api,Jar:camel-core -xml,Jar:camel-core-osgi,Jar:jaxb-core,Jar:jaxb-impl [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : No translation found for macro: body [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : No translation found for macro: sys.runtime.id [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Superfluous export-package instructions: [io.fabric8.*] [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for io.fabric8.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.apache.camel.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.apache.commons.logging.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.apache.felix.gogo.commands.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.apache.felix.service.command.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.osgi.service.component.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.springframework.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for org.springframework.osgi.* [WARNING] Bundle org.jboss.quickstarts.fuse:beginner-camel-log:bundle:6.2.0.redh at-109 : Did not find matching referal for scala* [INFO] [INFO] maven-source-plugin:2.2.1:jar-no-fork (attach-sources) @ beginner-cam el-log [INFO] [INFO] maven-install-plugin:2.4:install (default-install) @ beginner-camel-l og [INFO] Installing C:\Fuse\jboss-fuse-6.2.0.redhat-109\quickstarts\beginner\camel -log\target\shared.jar to C:\Users\aileenc\.m2\repository\org\jboss\quickstarts\ fuse\beginner-camel-log\6.2.0.redhat-109\beginner-camel-log-6.2.0.redhat-109.jar [INFO] Installing C:\Fuse\jboss-fuse-6.2.0.redhat-109\quickstarts\beginner\camel -log\pom.xml to C:\Users\aileenc\.m2\repository\org\jboss\quickstarts\fuse\begin (default-install) @ beginner-camel- log [INFO] Local OBR update disabled (enable with -DobrRepository) [INFO] [INFO] <<< fabric8-maven-plugin:1.2.0.redhat-109:deploy (default-cli) @ beginner amel-log\pom.xml Uploading: http://samsung_rv511:8181/maven/upload/org/jboss/quickstarts/fuse/beg inner-camel-log/6.2.0.redhat-109/beginner-camel-log-6.2.0.redhat-109.pom [INFO] buginformation.summary: Fuse and A-MQ both have the amq:(esb:)check-for-user and amq:(esb:)create-admin-user
buginformation.description:
IMHO Fuse should just contain: esb:create-admin-user esb:check-for-user and A-MQ should just contain: amq:create-admin-user amq:check-for-user
JBossA-MQ:karaf@root> esb:check-for-user
No user found in etc/users.properties. Please use the &apos;esb:create-admin-user&apos;
command to create one.
JBossFuse:karaf@root> amq:create-admin-user
No user specified. Some features may not be accessible.

buginformation.summary: Bump licence headers to 2016
Please update file name from jboss-fuse-full to jboss-fuse-karaf
buginformation.description: This was meant to be done for 6.2.1 but did not happen. Please correct for 6.3
